# Test Plan

## 1. Document Control

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0.0 | 2026-02-02 | QA Lead | Initial template creation |

---

## 2. Scope and Objectives

### 2.1 Purpose
This test plan defines the testing approach for [RELEASE/SPRINT/FEATURE] of [PROJECT NAME], including scope, schedule, resources, and deliverables.

### 2.2 Scope

#### In Scope
| Feature/Component | Test Level | Priority |
|-------------------|------------|----------|
| [Feature 1] | Unit, Integration, E2E | P0 |
| [Feature 2] | Integration, E2E | P1 |
| [API Endpoint] | Integration, Contract | P0 |
| [UI Component] | Unit, E2E, Accessibility | P1 |

#### Out of Scope
| Feature/Component | Reason |
|-------------------|--------|
| [Feature X] | Covered by vendor SLA |
| [Legacy Module] | Scheduled for deprecation |
| [Third-party Integration] | External team responsibility |

### 2.3 Test Objectives
- [ ] Validate all acceptance criteria for in-scope features
- [ ] Achieve minimum 80% code coverage for new code
- [ ] Ensure zero critical or high security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Verify performance meets SLA targets
- [ ] Confirm cross-browser compatibility

---

## 3. Test Strategy Summary

### 3.1 Test Levels and Approach

| Test Level | Approach | Automation | Owner |
|------------|----------|------------|-------|
| Unit | White-box, developer-driven | 100% | Developers |
| Integration | API contract, database | 100% | QA + Dev |
| E2E | User journey, critical path | 100% | QA |
| Security | SAST, DAST, dependency scan | Partial | Security Team |
| Performance | Load, stress, soak | 100% | SRE |
| Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance | Partial | QA |

### 3.2 Test Environment Requirements

| Environment | Configuration | Data Strategy |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|
| Test | [OS/Browser versions] | Synthetic test data |
| Staging | Production-like | Sanitized production clone |
| Production | Live (smoke tests only) | Read-only verification |

---

## 4. Test Cases

### 4.1 Test Case Summary

| ID | Scenario | Priority | Type | Owner | Status |
|----|----------|----------|------|-------|--------|
| TC-001 | [Critical user login flow] | P0 | E2E | [Name] | Draft |
| TC-002 | [API authentication] | P0 | Integration | [Name] | Draft |
| TC-003 | [Data validation] | P1 | Unit | [Name] | Draft |
| TC-004 | [Error handling] | P1 | E2E | [Name] | Draft |

### 4.2 Detailed Test Cases

#### TC-001: [Critical User Flow Name]
**Priority:** P0  
**Type:** End-to-End  
**Requirement Reference:** [REQ-XXX]  

**Preconditions:**
- User account exists with valid credentials
- Test environment accessible
- Database seeded with test data

**Test Steps:**
| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Navigate to login page | Login form displayed |
| 2 | Enter valid credentials | Authentication successful |
| 3 | Complete MFA challenge | Dashboard displayed |
| 4 | Navigate to [feature] | Feature loads correctly |
| 5 | Perform [action] | Action completes successfully |

**Postconditions:**
- User session active
- Audit log entry created
- Test data remains consistent

**Test Data:**
- Username: `test.user@example.com`
- Password: [From test vault]
- MFA Code: Generated by test authenticator

---

#### TC-002: [API Endpoint Test]
**Priority:** P0  
**Type:** Integration  
**Requirement Reference:** [REQ-XXX]  

**Preconditions:**
- API service running
- Authentication token available
- Test database initialized

**Test Steps:**
| Step | Action | Expected Result |
|------|--------|-----------------|
| 1 | Send GET request to `/api/v1/resource` | 200 OK response |
| 2 | Verify response schema | Matches OpenAPI spec |
| 3 | Check response time | < 200ms |
| 4 | Validate pagination | Correct page info returned |

**Expected Response:**
```json
{
  "data": [...],
  "pagination": {
    "page": 1,
    "per_page": 20,
    "total": 100
  }
}
```

---

## 5. Test Data Requirements

### 5.1 Data Sources
| Data Type | Source | Volume | Refresh Frequency |
|-----------|--------|--------|-------------------|
| User accounts | Synthetic generation | 100 | Per test run |
| Product catalog | Production subset | 1000 | Weekly |
| Transaction history | Synthetic | 10000 | Per test run |
| Configuration | Version controlled | N/A | On change |

### 5.2 Data Management
- PII must be masked or synthetic
- Test data isolated per environment
- Data cleanup procedures defined
- Referential integrity maintained

---

## 6. Entry and Exit Criteria

### 6.1 Entry Criteria (Test Execution Start)
- [ ] Code complete and deployed to test environment
- [ ] Unit tests passing (>80% coverage)
- [ ] Test environment stable and accessible
- [ ] Test data prepared and validated
- [ ] Test cases reviewed and approved
- [ ] Known defects documented

### 6.2 Exit Criteria (Test Execution Complete)
- [ ] All P0 test cases executed and passed
- [ ] P1 test cases >= 95% pass rate
- [ ] No open critical or high defects
- [ ] Code coverage >= 80% for new code
- [ ] Performance tests meet SLA targets
- [ ] Security scan shows no critical issues
- [ ] Test summary report completed

### 6.3 Suspension Criteria
Testing will be suspended if:
- Critical defect blocks major functionality
- Test environment becomes unstable
- > 20% of automated tests failing due to infrastructure issues

---

## 9. Risk Management

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Environment instability | Medium | High | Dedicated test environment, monitoring |
| Resource unavailability | Low | High | Cross-training, backup assignments |
| Scope creep | Medium | Medium | Change control process |
| Test data issues | Low | Medium | Data validation, synthetic data |
| Tool failures | Low | Medium | Alternative tools identified |
